scopes.https://www.googleapis.com/auth/colabora: Read, write, and delete your Colab resources
Read, write, and delete your Colab resources

prettyPrint.name: $prettyPrint
prettyPrint.description: Returns response with indentations and line breaks.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. This value is unused.

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

details.description:
A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
message types for APIs to use.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

message.description:
A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

Status.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
[API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

runtimeSpecs.description: The list of runtime specs available to the requesting user.

value.description: The exception message, e.g. "name 'foo' is not defined".

traceback.description: The formatted traceback, one frame per entry.

Error.description: A Python exception raised during execution.

BaseOperation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

name.description:
The server-assigned name, which is only unique within the same service that
originally returns it. If you use the default HTTP mapping, the
`name` should be a resource name ending with `operations/{unique_id}`.

error.description: The error result of the operation in case of failure or cancellation.

done.description:
If the value is `false`, it means the operation is still in progress.
If `true`, the operation is completed, and either `error` or `response` is
available.

x-google-enum-descriptions: Default value. This value is unused.
User is not subscribed to any paid tier.
User is subscribed to Colab Pro.
User is subscribed to Colab Pro+.
Default value. This value is unused.

name.description: Identifier. Name of the subscription. This will always be "subscription".

runtimes.description: The list of runtimes assigned to the requesting user.

Key.description: Key fields that uniquely identify a runtime spec.

accelerator.description:
Required. Runtime accelerator model name (e.g., "T4", "L4", "A100").
For CPU runtime specs, this field will be "NONE".

key.description: Required. The key of the runtime spec.

eligible.description: Output only. Whether the requesting user is eligible for the runtime spec.

ExecuteCodeResult.description:
The result of an `ExecuteCode` call, returned as the terminal
`structured_content` of the response stream.

richOutputsDropped.description:
Whether the execution produced rich (non-text) outputs that this tool does
not surface (e.g. images, Plotly/Vega figures, widgets). Always false for
executions that produced only text.

executionCount.description:
The Jupyter `In[N]` execution count assigned by the kernel. Informational
only. Not for addressing executions, for that use `execution_id`.

session.description:
The session the execution ran in.
Format: `runtimes/{runtime}/sessions/{session}`.

result.description:
The `text/plain` representation of the last expression's value, if the
execution ended on an expression. Empty otherwise.

executionId.description:
The execution identifier this result is for, echoed from the request. Empty
when the request supplied no `execution_id` (such an execution is not
resumable). Use it to resume.

outputTruncated.description:
Whether the runtime truncated buffered output (size or line caps hit). When
true, `stdout`/`stderr` are tail-truncated.

executionError.description:
Populated when the execution raised. Mutually exclusive with a successful
completion.

CreateRuntimeOperation.description: This resource represents a long-running operation where metadata and response fields are strongly typed.

Operation.description:
This resource represents a long-running operation that is the result of a
network API call.

metadata.description:
Service-specific metadata associated with the operation.  It typically
contains progress information and common metadata such as create time.
Some services might not provide such metadata.  Any method that returns a
long-running operation should document the metadata type, if any.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

response.description:
The normal, successful response of the operation.  If the original
method returns no data on success, such as `Delete`, the response is
`google.protobuf.Empty`.  If the original method is standard
`Get`/`Create`/`Update`, the response should be the resource.  For other
methods, the response should have the type `XxxResponse`, where `Xxx`
is the original method name.  For example, if the original method name
is `TakeSnapshot()`, the inferred response type is
`TakeSnapshotResponse`.

additionalProperties.description: Properties of the object. Contains field @type with type URL.

connectionInfo.description: Output only. The connection info used to authenticate to the runtime.

runtimeSpec.description: Required. The specification of the runtime.

name.description:
Identifier. The name of the runtime in the format `runtimes/{runtime_id}`, where
`runtime_id` is either the value supplied via
`CreateRuntimeRequest.runtime_id` or, if none was supplied, a random UUID
generated by the backend.

ConnectionInfo.description: Connection info used to authenticate to the runtime.

token.description:
Output only. The runtime proxy token. This is a short-lived credential used to
authenticate to the runtime.

expireTime.description: Output only. The expiration time of the runtime proxy token.

url.description: Output only. The authenticated URL that can be used to connect to the runtime.

runtime.description:
The runtime resource being created, in the format of
`runtimes/{runtime_id}`.

get.description: Gets the subscription of the requesting user.
Gets a Colab runtime assigned to the requesting user.

parameters.name: runtime
parameters.description: Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

parameters.name: runtime
parameters.description: Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

get.description: Lists Colab runtimes assigned to the requesting user.

requestBody.description: Required. Specifications for the runtime to assign.

parameters.name: requestId
parameters.description:
Optional. A unique identifier for this request.

This request is only idempotent if a `request_id` is provided.
See https://google.aip.dev/155 for more details.

If provided, the request ID must be in UUID4 format per
https://linter.aip.dev/155/request-id-format.

parameters.name: runtimeId
parameters.description:
Optional. A unique identifier for the runtime. If not supplied, a random UUID will be
generated. If a runtime with the given ID owned by the requesting user
already exists, a completed Operation with an ALREADY_EXISTS error will be
returned.
See https://google.aip.dev/133#user-specified-ids for more details.

The ID must conform to https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc1034,
specifically:
- 1 to 63 characters
- Lower-case letters, digits, and hyphens
- Start with a letter
- End with a letter or digit

get.description: Lists Colab runtime specs available to the requesting user.
